assessment_items.question_content:
***Ara Pacis* is one of the most important monuments from Augustan Rome. It was commissioned by the Roman Senate to commemorate the *Pax Romana,* the "Roman peace."** 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/34476bc9ce1444f64c430205b971a845.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **This statement is correct. The *Ara Pacis* is one of the most important monuments from Ancient Rome. It was commissioned by the Roman Senate to commemorate the Pax Romana that was established by Augustus.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Augustus is said to have found Rome a city of brick and left it a city of granite, evoking an image of the golden age of Rome.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The Roman historian Seutonius wrote that Augustus found Rome a city of brick and left it a city of marble, evoking an image of the golden age of Rome.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Art historians interpret  the numerous plants and animals to represent  [[☃ dropdown 1]] of the Augustan golden age.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/023b79026c0d93d37ee3bba0a3b4f64a.jpg)
hints: **Art historians interpret  the numerous plants and animals to represent the fertility and abundance of the Augustan golden age.**

choices.content: trade with southern parts of the empire

assessment_items.question_content:
**The reliefs on the exterior precinct walls of the *Ara Pacis* may refer to all of the following EXCEPT . . .**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/34476bc9ce1444f64c430205b971a845.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The narrative panels may refer to Aeneas, the ancestors of Augustus, and personifications of Rome and Peace, though art historians disagree about the precise interpretation of these scenes.**

choices.content: Aeneas, one of Rome's legendary founders

assessment_items.question_content:
**In many ways the relief sculpture of the procession on either side of the Ara Pacis recalls the frieze on the Parthenon, recalling the golden age of Greece. However, it differs in which respects?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **In many ways the relief sculpture of the procession on either side of the Ara Pacis recalls the frieze on the Parthenon, recalling the golden age of Greece. However, it differs in that it likely depicts a specific event on a specific date and portraits of Romans.**

choices.content: it likely depicts a specific event on a specific date
it likely depicts the Roman re-enactment of the pan-athenaic procession
it likely depicts the initiation ritual for Roman cult of the state
